{, 1T Y NEWS
~ N BR E F ~

pages.raw_text:
Reports and comparisons of tho
estimates of the timber cruisers
and locaters of the Occur d'Alene
| Indian reservation were made last
night at a meeting of the Occur
d'Alene settlers association at the
First UniversaliSt church.


A telephone message from Miles,
Wash, yesterday was the cause of
the arrest in Spokane of an Indian
buck and young squaw who had
left the Spokane reservation with
out permission.


A dispute over 40 cents, the diff
ference in the price offered and de-
manded for a rug, resulted yester
day in a settlement for $11.10 in
Justice Suckers court. The case
was that of Otto Gunderson against
E. Z. Smith, who purchased the rug.


Through some oversight suit was
brought against the wrong railroad
yesterday in Judge Remnants court.
Lee Denman sued the Northern Pa
cific: instead it was the Spokane,
Portland & Seattle road upon which
he was injured.


Frances Penkake yesterday filed
suit in the United States circuit
court against the Great Northern
for $50,0 for the death of her hus-
band. Owen L., who was killed on
the road while acting as freight
conductor.


For $17,5O0 Dr.. William Sellers
has purchased from J. L. Warn so
foot frontage on the north side of
Third avenue, near Division. A
frame apartment on the place now
produces a rental of $500 monthly.


A temporary injunction delaying
the work on the Brownies addition
paving project was yesterday
granted by Judge Hinkle.


The sale of the northwest corner
of Fifth avenue and Sherman street
